hyperedges.label: Legal Rulings Block (Prayer, Fasting, Hajj, Marriage, Divorce, Debt, Interest, Charity, Dietary Laws)
Bani Israel Narrative Arc (Exodus, Covenant, Cow, Disobedience)
Opening Contrast Block (Believers, Hypocrites, Disbelievers verses 1-20)
Legal rulings cluster (inheritance, marriage, food, ablution)
Narratives of Mary and Jesus across surahs 3-5
Comparative religion / People of the Book engagement
Prophet-narratives cluster in Al-A'raf (Noah, Hud, Lot)
Battle of Badr context in Al-Anfal (event, divine aid, spoils)
Surah 9 unique features (no bismillah, repentance, hypocrites exposed)
Joseph's complete narrative arc (dream to reunion)
Al-Kahf's four-story structure (Cave, Gardens, Khidr, Dhul-Qarnayn)
Surahs named after prophets and figures (Yunus, Hud, Yusuf, Ibrahim, Maryam, Ta-Ha)
Solomon's narrative arc across surahs
Hawamim cluster - surahs opening with Ha-Meem (40-46)
Ya-Sin structural significance - heart of the Quran
Ar-Rahman 31-Refrain Pattern: 'fa-bi-ayyi alaa-i rabbikuma tukadhdhiban' repeats across the surah binding creation, judgment, and paradise
Medinan Legal & Community-Conduct Cluster (community rules, divorce, oaths, hypocrites, allies, exile)
Early Meccan Revelation-Onset Cluster (the cloaked one, the enwrapped, the pen, Noah's preaching, command to arise and warn)
Juz Amma - 37 short rhythmic surahs from An-Naba through An-Nas
Al-Mu'awwidhatan - the twin protection surahs
Revelation arc - first revealed (Al-Alaq) and last revealed (An-Nasr)
Abrahamic Prophets
Jacob's Line
Exodus Narrative
Nativity Narrative
Divine Names of Bismillah
Prophets to Destroyed Nations
Lesser-Mentioned Prophets
Holy Cities of Arabia
Creedal Core Themes
Social and Legal Themes
Narrative and Warning Themes
Numerical Structural Miracles
Structural Patterns
